factors.summary: The company is increasing its capital expenditures, reflecting confidence in future growth. Approximately 50 percent of our capital expenditures are expected to be invested outside of the U.S. in 2015.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different segments and regions, with some areas experiencing growth while others face declines. The North American on-highway markets have shown improvement, driving sales increases in the Engine and Components segments. However, other markets, such as power generation and off-highway mining, have seen weaker demand. Additionally, emerging markets are seeking advanced technologies to meet stricter emissions requirements, which has increased demand for the company's products in regions like Europe and China.

evidence.text: Demand trends are mixed across different segments and regions, with some businesses experiencing growth while others face declines.

Worldwide revenues improved 11 percent in 2014 compared to 2013, primarily due to the improvements in North American on-highway demand and the consolidation of partially owned North American distributors.

evidence.text: Engine segment sales decreased slightly versus 2012 and was primarily driven by the following: •Engine segment sales decreased by 7 percent due to lower demand in the power generation markets, weaker demand in the North American heavy-duty truck markets and continued weakness in industrial demand, primarily in off-highway mining markets.

As our customers in emerging markets continue to grow in size and scope, they are increasingly seeking to export their products to other countries. This has meant greater demand for our advanced engine technologies to help these customers meet the more stringent emissions requirements of developed markets, as well as greater demand for access to our distribution systems for purposes of equipment servicing.

Higher compensation expense was primarily due to increased headcount to support our strategic growth initiatives.
Selling, general and administrative expenses increased primarily due to higher compensation and related expenses of $118 million and increased consulting fees of $28 million.

factors.summary: Productivity has shown improvement across the business, driven by cost reduction initiatives and operational efficiency gains. However, there is a noted decline in gross margin due to lower demand for certain products, offsetting some of the positive impacts.

evidence.text: The decrease in gross margin as a percentage of sales was primarily due to a decline in demand for high-horsepower engines and gensets, partially offset by improved price realization, lower material and commodity costs and the absence of 2012 restructuring charges of $29 million.
The improved gross margin was the result of higher volumes, improved Distribution segment sales related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2012.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in Distribution and Components, which have seen significant increases. However, there are also declines in lower-margin segments like Engine and Power Generation. The growth in higher-margin segments is driven by factors such as consolidation of distributors, increased demand in emission solutions, and expansion in key markets like North America and Brazil.

evidence.text: Distribution segment sales increased by 14 percent primarily due to incremental sales in 2013 related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since June 2012.

Components segment sales increased by 8 percent due to higher sales within the emission solutions business, mainly related to improved on-highway OEM and aftermarket demand in North America, a full year of sales from Hilite which was acquired in the third quarter of 2012, 2013 pre-buy activity in anticipation of the Euro VI emission standards and growth in the medium-duty Brazilian truck market resulting in improved aftertreatment demand.
The Components segment sales increased by 18 percent primarily due to higher demand in on-highway markets in North America, Europe, and China.

factors.summary: The company faces environmental compliance costs, particularly due to new emission standards, but is managing these through product development and investments in emission solutions. While the costs are significant, the company appears well-positioned to meet regulatory requirements.

evidence.text: Environmental compliance costs are a consideration, particularly in the context of new emission standards, but the company is managing these through product development.
Environmental regulations are creating significant compliance costs, particularly in emission solutions, but the company is well-positioned to meet these requirements.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing positive market share trends across multiple regions, particularly in North America and China, driven by product differentiation and strategic initiatives.

evidence.text: Medium-duty truck engine sales increased due to market share gains in the North American medium-duty truck market and improved demand in the Brazilian and European truck markets.
We expect the new ISG engine...to continue to gain market share in China in its first full year of production.

factors.summary: The company is strengthening its competitive advantages through product innovation and market leadership, particularly in emission solutions and aftertreatment systems, which are driving market share gains. The focus on developing new products to meet future emission standards and improvements in fuel economy performance further supports this differentiation.

evidence.text: Components segment sales increased by 8 percent due to higher sales within the emission solutions business, mainly related to improved on-highway OEM and aftermarket demand in North America, a full year of sales from Hilite which was acquired in the third quarter of 2012, 2013 pre-buy activity in anticipation of the Euro VI emission standards and growth in the medium-duty Brazilian truck market resulting in improved aftertreatment demand.
Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world and improvements in fuel economy performance.
